manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: jacks, pear, watermelon, stairs, opossum, window, meteorology, professor, cello.
Your opponent's team words are: train, plum, tie, salesperson, harp, ceiling, basement, lion.
Distractor words are: blueberry, violin, puzzle, medicine, top, actor, cougar.
The kill words are: hat.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words. Must not be any of the listed words or contain parts of them. Provide clue and list of target words (the ones we intend them to guess). Should be short.

We have our words: jacks, pear, watermelon, stairs, opress? actually opossum, window, meteorology, professor, cello.

We want to maximize number but plausible.

Clue must be semantically related to target words. Could be "school"? relates to professor, maybe stairs? maybe jacks? Not good.

"Fruit" could relate to pear and watermelon. Also blueberry is a distractor fruit but that's fine; but blueberry is a distractor, clue "fruit" might cause opponent to guess blueberry (distractor) - not ideal but maybe okay. Could be "music" relates to cello, maybe jacks? Not.

"Academic" relates to professor, meteorology (science), maybe? Could be "science" for meteorology, professor, maybe window? Not.

"House" relates to window, stairs, maybe jacks? Not.

"Animal" relates to opossum, maybe jacks? Not.

"String" relates to cello.

We could give clue "science" targeting meteorology, professor. Also maybe "weather"? That is meteorology only. Could also include "pear" is fruit. Let's think.

We can give clue "fruit" with targets: pear, watermelon. That's 2. Might be safe. But blueberry is distractor, and also "blueberry" is fruit, could cause opponent guess distractor. That risk.

Maybe clue "vegetation"? not.

Better maybe clue "lecture" targets professor, maybe meterology? Not.

Clue "music" could target cello, maybe jacks? Not. "Orchestra" maybe cello.

We could combine three: "college" - professor, maybe stairs (college building), maybe window (classroom). Might be ambiguous.

The clue must not contain parts of any listed words. Eg not "pear" or "cell". "Fruit" contains "pear"? No, but "fruit" contains "fruit". It's okay. Not part of any listed words.

We want to avoid risking opponent guessing kill word "hat". Good.

Select clue "academic" (targets professor, meteorology). Might be okay but meteorology is academic field. Could also include "stairs"? Not.

Maybe we go for "music" with cello only. But perhaps better to guess more.

Clue "garden" could relate to pear (tree), watermelon (vines), maybe also jacks? No.

Clue "weather" only meteorology.

Could we make clue "classroom"? relates to professor, window, stairs maybe. But classroom includes "room" which is part of "basement"? Not.

Better to give two-word clue? Must be single-word clue.

I think "fruit" with targets: pear, watermelon. Provide.

Check not containing any part of listed words. "fruit" contains "ru" etc not.

Ok answer
